description: The Cortesi brothers were four Italian-descended siblings who operated as gangsters in London in the early 20th century. They engaged in protection racketeering of gamblers and bookmakers, and were rivals of the Sabini family. The brothers were involved in a violent confrontation at the Fratellanza Social Club in 1922, leading to arrests and convictions. The group was active from the 1900s until the deaths of the brothers between 1938 and 1972.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Cortesi family moved to Paris , France , where all four brothers were born.
country_links.context: The Cortesi brothers were born in Paris, France, indicating the organization had a base there.

country_links.evidence_quote: became involved in protection racketeering of gamblers and bookmakers ... a confrontation at the Fratellanza Social Club at 23 Great Bath Street, Clerkenwell.
country_links.context: The Cortesi brothers operated in London, engaging in protection racketeering and violent confrontations.
